channel.title: +Precession records on ज्योतिषे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +Precession records on ज्योतिषे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.title: 0 Prehistory
item.description: The veda also furnishes direct accounts of the deadly jaw-like nature of the halves of the years …Thus, the crashing doors are the halves of the year held by the frame of solstices and equinoxes. … the deadly crashing doors are an ancient mythic motif representing the year and might be used in the precession myths. … A corollary would be that these mythic motifs had come into being at least by 12000 year BP when the Native Americans are believed to have separated from the Eurasians.

item.title: Early ayanAMsha offset records
item.description: The mAgha-solstice epoch was very influential in later literature. We see then evidence of offsetting nakshatras to match tropical months. Amara i. 4.13: द्वौ द्वौ माघादिमासौ स्याद् ऋतुस् तैर् अयनं त्रिभिः। Mah. Apu. 167, 26 and 28: परिवृत्तो हि भगवान् सहस्रांशुर् दिवाकरः ॥२६॥ … माघोऽयं समनुप्राप्तो मासः सौम्यो युधिष्ठिर॥ त्रिभागशेषः पक्षोऽयं शुक्लो भवितुम् अर्हति ॥२८॥

item.title: Record of pole stars
item.description: references - RNI2021-YT 3000 BCE when a-Draconis was the star Abhaya-Dhruva stationary at the tail end of the animal figure looking like a whale called Siśumāra recognizable as the modern constellation Draco. (taittirIya Aranyaka refers to this.) See shiMshumAra section in tArAmaNDala page for TA and later refereces. Marriage rituals assume a fixed dhruva - so should be from Thuban days. Indologist Jacobe agreed, others didn’t. एकाग्निकाण्डे विवाहप्रकरणे- ध्रु॒व-क्षि॑तिर् ध्रु॒व-यो॑निर्
